verdict_reasoning: VEGFA is a secreted heparin-binding homodimeric growth factor of the PDGF/VEGF family with no transmembrane domain or GPI anchor. It signals by binding VEGFR1/2/3 and neuropilins on endothelial cells — classic recruitment-test failure: the surface receptors are the anchors, VEGFA is in equilibrium with the soluble/heparin-bound pool. Clinical anti-VEGFA antibodies (bevacizumab, ranibizumab) target the circulating ligand, not a cell-surface form. Walking contextual buckets: no cell-state/cancer/cell-death outer-leaflet display of the protein body; no narrow germline/developmental surface lineage; not a lysosomal TM protein; no documented PM minority pool with its own anchor; heparin/HSPG and matrix binding is reversible non-covalent, not wash-resistant covalent TM tethering.
